channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pentagon releases names of 4 U.S. service members killed in Iran war
item.description: The Pentagon has identified four of the six U.S. service members killed in the first attacks on Iran.
item.content:encoded: The Pentagon has identified four of the six U.S. service members killed in the first attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Trump claims there would have been a nuclear war if U.S. didn't strike Iran first
item.description: President Trump offered a new reason Tuesday for the U.S. strikes on Iran, saying it was his opinion that the country was going to strike first.
item.content:encoded: President Trump offered a new reason Tuesday for the U.S. strikes on Iran, saying it was his opinion that the country was going to strike first.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Rep. Gregory Meeks on U.S. operations against Iran and the vote on Trump's war powers
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Rep. Gregory Meeks of New York, ranking member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, about U.S. military operations against Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Rep. Gregory Meeks of New York, ranking member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, about U.S. military operations against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: White House begins arranging flights for Americans stranded in the Middle East
item.description: The Trump administration says it is now starting to arrange military and charter flights to help Americans who have been stranded in the Middle East since the war with Iran began.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration says it is now starting to arrange military and charter flights to help Americans who have been stranded in the Middle East since the war with Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Fears begin to mount for Iranians held prisoner by the regime as war enters fifth day
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Would you go to a play by yourself? Theaters are hoping so
item.description: As solo leisure booms globally, theater operator ATG Entertainment is launching "Solo Seats" events to destigmatize going to the theater alone.
item.content:encoded:
As solo leisure booms globally, theater operator ATG Entertainment is launching "Solo Seats" events to destigmatize going to the theater alone.

(Image credit: Roger Mastroianni)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel continues airstrikes, as Iran widens regional attacks, Trump claims Iran would have struck first if U.S. had not acted, results are in for several U.S. Senate primaries ahead of the midterms.
item.content:encoded: Israel continues airstrikes, as Iran widens regional attacks, Trump claims Iran would have struck first if U.S. had not acted, results are in for several U.S. Senate primaries ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Family sues after researcher's death, alleging university's probe led to suicide
item.description: The suicide of a Chinese American cancer researcher has raised painful questions over alleged discrimination against Asian scientists in the U.S.
item.content:encoded: The suicide of a Chinese American cancer researcher has raised painful questions over alleged discrimination against Asian scientists in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Gas prices jump and stocks plunge as fallout from the war with Iran spreads
item.description: Oil prices are up and stock prices are down, as financial markets react to the U.S. war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Oil prices are up and stock prices are down, as financial markets react to the U.S. war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Padma Lakshmi returns to cooking competition shows with 'America's Culinary Cup'
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel talks to television host and author Padma Lakshmi about her new cooking competition show, "America's Culinary Cup."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel talks to television host and author Padma Lakshmi about her new cooking competition show, "America's Culinary Cup."
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel
